data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Apple Inc. ("Apple Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Apple announced its upcoming release of the new iPhone 16 and 16 Plus models on June 10, 2024. The Company heavily marketed these new iPhones as being "built for Apple Intelligence," a suite of generative AI features integrated into iOS 18 and its devices, including a major upgrade to Siri, Apple's voice assistant. Apple touted the new, upgraded AI-driven Siri as "more conversational," capable of increased understanding of user context, and being powered by ChatGPT, a state-of-the-art natural language processing model developed by OpenAI. Apple claimed that the iPhone 16 and 16 Plus were designed from the ground up to support Apple Intelligence, redefining smartphone use in the new AI economy.

Then, Apple announced on March 7, 2025, that the availability of Apple Intelligence would be significantly delayed, and that the Company "needed more time to finish developing the new Siri features" that would enhance Siri's AI capabilities and anticipated "rolling them out in the coming year."

Following this news, the price of Apple common stock fell over 5% on March 10, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

data.title: UK Watchdog's Review Finds 'Most' Smaller Asset Managers Meet Regulatory Standards
data.description:
The UK Financial Conduct Authority's review of smaller asset management companies found that most of them are meeting regulatory expectations, according to a Thursday release.

The FCA's findings focused on high-risk investments, conflicts of interest and the consumer duty. While most of the companies clearly categorized high-risk investments and made "good" progress in implementing the consumer duty, some lacked sufficient processes to ensure high-risk investments were sold to clients only when appropriate, the regulator said.

On the other hand, "many" companies were found to have ineffective arrangements for managing conflicts of interest, particularly in those where senior staff held multiple roles.

The regulator said it will continue to monitor and work with companies that need to make improvements to ensure compliance.

data.title: Earnings Flash (PAA) Plains All American Pipeline LP Reports Q1 Revenue $12.01B, vs. FactSet Est of $14.14B
Stocks Rise Pre-Bell as Investors Parse US-UK Agreement, Await China Trade Talks
data.description:
The benchmark US stock measures were pointing higher before the open Friday amid optimism after Washington struck an agreement with the UK, while investors await an expected meeting between US and Chinese officials over the weekend.

The S&P 500 rose 0.3%, the Nasdaq added 0.4%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average ticked up 0.1% in premarket activity. The indexes closed Thursday's trading session higher, representing the second consecutive day of gains.

US President Donald Trump on Thursday announced a trade agreement with the UK. As part of the deal, the US will raise $6 billion in external revenue from 10% tariffs, $5 billion in "new export opportunities" while creating an aluminum and steel trading zone, and a "secure" pharmaceutical supply chain, Trump said in a social media post.

"Many more (deals) to come," according to Trump.

"While exemptions will nibble away at the effective tariff rate, the baseline 10% is not going anywhere," Oxford Economics said in a Thursday note. "The average US tariff is still set to remain in double digits, which will deliver a big hit to real incomes in the US, causing growth to slow sharply in the second half of the year."

US and Chinese officials are due to meet in Switzerland over the weekend to discuss economic and trade matters. "We're gonna have a good weekend with China," Trump said Thursday, according to The Wall Street Journal.

The two-year US Treasury yield retreated 2.7 basis points to 3.87% in premarket action, while the 10-year rate inclined 0.5 basis points to 4.38%.

With no major economic data scheduled for Friday, traders will focus on commentary from several key central bank officials for clues on future monetary policy and economic outlook, including Federal Reserve Governors Michael Barr, Adriana Kugler, and Christopher Waller. New York Fed President John Williams is also scheduled to speak today.

The Federal Open Market Committee kept interest rates in the range of 4.25% to 4.50% for a third straight meeting on Wednesday and flagged rising uncertainty about the economic outlook.

Shares of Pinterest jumped 14% pre-bell as the image-sharing platform company issued an upbeat second-quarter revenue outlook. Monster Beverage declined 2.2% as the company's first-quarter sales missed estimates. Lyft climbed 8.4% as the ride-hailing company surprisingly swung to a first-quarter profit.

Enbridge , EchoStar and Essent Group report their latest financial results before the bell, among others. Hawaiian Electric Industries posts earnings after the markets close.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil increased 1.9% to $61.07 a barrel before the opening bell. The weekly Baker Hughes domestic oil-and-gas rig count is due at 1 pm ET.

Gold edged 0.7% higher to $3,329 per troy ounce. Bitcoin advanced 2% to $103,180.

The Company will host a conference call to discuss its corporate and financial results on Friday, May 16, 2025 at 10:00am ET.

The dial-in for the conference call is (888)-506-0062 or (973)-528-0011 for international callers. The participant code is 903493. A live webcast can also be accessed through the following link: https://www.webcaster4.com/Webcast/Page/3029/52355.

A replay of the conference call will be available on the SWK Holdings website shortly following the call.

About SWK Holdings Corporation

SWK Holdings Corporation is a life science focused specialty finance company partnering with small- and mid-sized commercial-stage healthcare companies. SWK provides non-dilutive financing to fuel the development and commercialization of lifesaving and life-enhancing medical technologies and products. SWK's unique financing structures provide flexible financing solutions at an attractive cost of capital to create long-term value for all SWK stakeholders. SWK's solutions include structured debt, traditional royalty monetization, synthetic royalty transactions, and asset purchases typically ranging in size from $5.0 million to $25.0 million. SWK also owns Enteris BioPharma, a clinical development and manufacturing organization providing development services to pharmaceutical partners as well as innovative formulation solutions built around its proprietary oral drug delivery technologies. data.title: Trade Desk Shares Climb After 1Q Revenue Surge
data.description:
By Dean Seal

Shares of Trade Desk jumped after the company reported a strong first quarter from soaring revenue.

The stock was up 14% at 68.55 in premarket trading. Shares had fallen 49% year-to-date when the market closed Thursday.

The platform for buyers of advertising posted a first-quarter profit of $50.7 million, or 10 cents a share, compared with $32 million, or 6 cents a share, in the same quarter a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, adjusted earnings were 33 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had been expecting 25 cents a share.

Revenue surged 25% to $616 million, topping analyst forecasts for $575.3 million, according to FactSet.

Trade Desk is guiding for at least $682 million in revenue for the current quarter, just under analyst estimates for $683.7 million.

The company said volatility in the economy has made 2025 an important year for marketers looking to use the open internet to drive business differentiation and growth.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: TeraWulf 1Q Loss Widens After Bitcoin Halving Cuts Revenue
data.description:
By Dean Seal

TeraWulf said its loss in the first quarter widened as revenue sank due to rising power prices and the halving of Bitcoin last year.

The bitcoin-mining company posted a loss of $61.4 million, compared with a loss of $9.6 million in the same quarter a year earlier.

Per share, the loss was 16 cents. Analysts polled by FactSet had been expecting a loss of 7 cents a share.

Revenue fell 19% to $34.4 million, below analyst projections for $41.3 million, according to FactSet.

The decline was caused by headwinds from the April 2024 halving of Bitcoin, a change occurring every four years that halves the number of bitcoins released on average every 10 minutes.

Increased network difficulty and rising power prices also cut into the top line, partially offset by a higher average bitcoin price and expanded mining capacity.

TeraWulf also said Friday that its board has refreshed an authorization for a $200 million stock-repurchase program.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Sylvamo Delivers Results In Line With Outlook, Well Positioned With Strong Balance Sheet
data.description:
Sylvamo, the global paper company, has released its first quarter 2025 earnings report, revealing a mixed performance compared to the previous quarter. The company, which operates mills in Europe, Latin America, and North America, has faced several operational challenges but remains optimistic about its future outlook.

Financial Highlights

For the first quarter of 2025, Sylvamo reported a net income of $27 million, translating to $0.65 per diluted share, a significant drop from the $81 million ($1.94 per diluted share) reported in the fourth quarter of 2024. Adjusted operating earnings also saw a decline, coming in at $28 million ($0.68 per diluted share) compared to $82 million ($1.96 per diluted share) in the previous quarter. Adjusted EBITDA was $90 million, representing an 11% margin, down from $157 million and a 16% margin in the fourth quarter of 2024. Cash provided by operating activities was $23 million, a sharp decrease from $164 million in the previous quarter, and free cash flow was negative at $(25) million, compared to $100 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company faced several challenges in the first quarter, including a $10 million unfavorable price and mix impact due to paper price decreases in Europe and Brazilian export regions. Volume decreased by $30 million, attributed to seasonally weak demand in Latin America and operational challenges in North America. Additionally, operations and other costs increased by $12 million, driven by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates and operational issues in North America. Planned maintenance outage expenses rose by $9 million, and input and transportation costs increased by $6 million due to higher energy prices and extreme cold weather across the United States.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Sylvamo returned nearly $40 million in cash to shareholders in the first quarter, distributing $18 million via dividends and repurchasing $20 million in shares. The company has $62 million remaining on its $150 million share repurchase authorization from September 2023. Additionally, the board of directors declared a second-quarter dividend of $0.45 per share, which was paid on April 29.

In terms of leadership changes, Jean-Michel Ribiéras, the current Chairman and CEO, announced his retirement at the end of the year. John Sims, who served as the company's first senior vice president and CFO, will take over as CEO on January 1, 2026. Don Devlin has been appointed as the new senior vice president and CFO, effective May 1.

Management's Perspective

Jean-Michel Ribiéras highlighted the completion of a heavy planned maintenance outage schedule in Europe and North America during the first quarter. He noted that the second quarter would be the heaviest outage quarter of the year, with over 80% of the total annual planned maintenance outage costs expected to be spent by mid-year. Ribiéras also emphasized the company's strong balance sheet, with a 1.1x leverage ratio and no major maturities until 2027, allowing Sylvamo to navigate the uncertain economic environment.

Future Outlook

For the second quarter, Sylvamo expects adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $75 million to $95 million. The company anticipates improvements in price and mix, operations and other costs, and input and transportation costs. However, planned maintenance outage expenses are expected to increase by $36 million. Management expects quarterly earnings to significantly improve in the second half of the year, benefiting from lower planned maintenance outage expenses, improved commercial results, and better operations.

SEC Filing: Sylvamo Corp [ SLVM ] - 8-K - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Open Lending Is Maintained at Buy by Needham
Anika Therapeutics Q1 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Anika Therapeutics, Inc., a global leader in osteoarthritis pain management and regenerative solutions, has released its financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company reported a mixed performance with notable achievements in its commercial channel and challenges in its OEM channel.

Financial Highlights

For the first quarter ended March 31, 2025, Anika reported revenue from continuing operations of $26.2 million, a 10% decrease compared to the same period in 2024. The company experienced a strong performance in its Commercial Channel, which saw an 18% increase in revenue, driven by the growth of the Integrity Implant System and international OA Pain Management. However, the OEM Channel revenue declined by 23% due to lower pricing for Monovisc® and Orthovisc® sold by its commercial partner, J&J MedTech.

Key financial metrics include:

  • Gross margin of 56%
  • Operating expenses decreased by 12% to $19.0 million
  • Loss from continuing operations of $4.0 million, or $0.28 per share
  • Adjusted net loss from continuing operations of $0.9 million, or $0.06 per share
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $0.1 million
  • Cash balance of $53.4 million

Business and Operational Highlights

The Integrity Implant System continued its sequential growth, with more than 300 surgeries performed for the second straight quarter. The company achieved 33% growth in its Regenerative Solutions portfolio, exceeding initial launch expectations for Integrity. Additionally, Anika saw a 13% growth in international OA Pain Management, driven by geographic expansion and market share gains for Cingal and Orthovisc.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During the quarter, Anika achieved significant regulatory and clinical milestones for its key pipeline programs. The company submitted the second PMA module for Hyalofast and received formal written feedback from the FDA regarding Cingal, clearing the path to NDA filing. Anika also completed a $15 million share repurchase plan in March, maintaining a strong financial position with $53 million in cash and no debt.

Management's Perspective

Cheryl Blanchard, President and CEO of Anika Therapeutics, commented on the company's performance: "Since our strategic realignment, we have outperformed in our Commercial Channel where we control sales, marketing, and pricing. We continue to see consistently strong growth within the Commercial Channel, up 18% in the quarter. Sales in our OEM Channel did not meet expectations due to lower pricing of Monovisc and Orthovisc in the U.S. Nevertheless, Monovisc and Orthovisc continue to hold leading positions in the U.S. market. I am also pleased to report that we continue to make excellent progress on our cost-saving measures, resulting in a 12% decline in operating expenses."

Future Outlook

Anika has updated its fiscal 2025 guidance, expecting Commercial Channel revenue to remain unchanged at $47 to $49.5 million, up 12% to 18% year-over-year. However, the OEM Channel revenue is expected to be lower, ranging from $62 to $65 million, down 16% to 20% year-over-year. The company has also revised its Adjusted EBITDA guidance to a range of -3% to 3%, down from the previous range of 8% to 10%, due to lower end-user pricing for Monovisc and Orthovisc, lower first half manufacturing yields, costs for the Cingal bioequivalence study, and the impacts of recently announced tariffs.

SEC Filing: Anika Therapeutics, Inc. [ ANIK ] - 8-K - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Britain increases wind farm incentives funding to $720 million
data.description:
Britain has more than doubled funding available under a scheme seeking to spur investment in offshore wind projects to 544 million pounds ($721.78 million), after higher than expected demand, the government said on Friday.

The country has put offshore wind at the heart of its 2030 clean energy plan and hopes to boost capacity from around 15 GW at present to 43-50 GW by the end of the decade.

But there are worries it could miss targets after one of the largest projects in development, Orsted's Hornsea 4 project, was cancelled this week. The Danish company said it was no longer economic to proceed given rising building costs.

An expansion of the investment incentive scheme which the government calls the 'Clean Industry Bonus' from an initial budget of 200 million pounds to 544 million pounds could help improve the financial viability of new projects.

The scheme will help underpin the economics of projects bidding in Britain's next annual renewable subsidy auction which will be held later this year.

Successful bidders in the Clean Industry Bonus will receive an initial 27 million pounds in funding for every gigawatt (GW) of capacity from offshore wind projects.

It is open to developers investing in regions that need it most or in cleaner supply chains, including traditional oil and gas communities, ex-industrial areas and ports and coastal towns, the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) said.

"This additional funding has the potential to help secure billions in private investment in new factories manufacturing components for the offshore wind industry across the UK," Ana Musat, Executive Director of Policy at industry group RenewableUK said in the government statement.

($1 = 0.7537 pounds)

data.title: Enbridge Q1 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Rise
data.description:
Enbridge (ENB) reported Q1 adjusted earnings Friday of CA$1.03 ($0.74) per share, up from CA$0.92 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet, on average, expected CA$0.96.

The Canadian pipeline firm's operating revenue for the quarter ended March 31 increased to CA$18.50 billion from CA$11.04 billion a year earlier. Four analysts expected CA$11.57 billion.

The company's shares were up 2% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Investors pull money out of US equity funds for a fourth straight week
data.description:
U.S. equity funds saw outflows for a fourth straight week through May 7, driven by uncertainties around trade tariffs and as investors awaited U.S.-China trade talks for more clues.

Investors withdrew a net $16.22 billion from U.S. equity funds during the week, the largest weekly net sales since March 19, data from LSEG Lipper showed.

A U.S. trade deal with Britain on Thursday, however, has fueled guarded optimism for progress in tariff talks with other countries. U.S. President Donald Trump also signaled that productive talks with China could lead to lower tariffs.

"We continue to view U.S. equities as attractive, with a year-end S&P 500 target of 5,800," said Mark Haefele, chief investment officer at UBS Global Wealth Management.

U.S. large-cap and mid-cap equity funds suffered net outflows of $13.6 billion and $1.12 billion, respectively, during the week.

U.S. small-cap equity fund outflows, meanwhile, eased to a six-week low of $917 million.

U.S. sectoral funds saw a net $2.89 billion worth of sales. Investors divested financials, tech, and metals and mining funds worth $1.18 billion, $507 million and $420 million, respectively.

Sentiment towards U.S. fixed-income markets improved during the week as fund investors poured a net $3.53 billion - the most in eight weeks - into U.S. bond funds.

Short-to-intermediate government and treasury funds saw a net $1.15 billion worth of purchases, reversing a net $765 million of sales the prior week. Municipal debt funds also saw a net $1.06 billion worth of additions.

At the same time, investors snapped up a net $28.4 billion worth of money market funds in their largest weekly net purchase since March 5.

data.description: This week, HDPE prices drifted lower in Latin America.An industry source in Latin America informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Import prices for high density polyethylene (HDPE) in Latin America continued their decline in the week concluded, with most grades experiencing reductions. This price decrease was primarily due to persistent competitive offers from US-sourced shipments, amidst a backdrop of sufficient regional supply and weak demand. While market sentiment was still pessimistic during the initial week of May, the outlook was not as dire as in April, with some market participants indicating that prices might be nearing a low point. Nevertheless, buyers remained prudent, delaying purchases in anticipation of clearer indications of market stabilization. CFR Brazil prices of HDPE Blow Moulding were assessed at the USD 890-920/mt levels, down USD (-10/mt) from last week.Meanwhile, CFR Brazil prices of HDPE Injection were assessed at the USD 900-930/mt levels while CFR Brazil prices of HDPE Film were assessed at the USD 950-960/mt levels, both constant from the previous week.CFR West Coast South America prices of HDPE Blow Moulding were assessed at the USD 880-910/mt levels, a week on week fall of USD (-20/mt). CFR West Coast South America prices of HDPE Film were assessed at the USD 920-950/mt levels while CFR West Coast South America prices of HDPE Injection were assessed at the USD 910-940/mt levels, both declined by USD (-20/mt) from last week.CFR Mercosur prices of HDPE Film were assessed at the USD 1070-1100/mt levels, lower by USD (-10/mt) from the previous week. CFR Mercosur prices HDPE Blow Moulding were assessed at the USD 1070-1100/mt levels while CFR Mercosur prices of HDPE Injection were assessed at the USD 1040-1070/mt levels, both down USD (-10/mt) from the previous week.Spot upstream ethylene prices on Thursday were assessed at the 19.50-20.00 cents/lb FOB U.S. Gulf levels, a week on week gain of (+1 cent/lb).In the US, HDPE blow moulding export prices at USD 775-795/mt FAS Houston levels while HDPE film export prices were assessed at USD 830-850/mt FAS Houston levels, both dropped by USD (-30/mt) from the previous week.Meanwhile, HDPE injection prices were assessed at the USD 820-840/mt FAS Houston levels, stable from last week.

data.title: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) export prices edge higher in the United States
data.description: This week, PVC export prices inched higher in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The US polyvinyl chloride (PVC) market has been facing ongoing supply constraints this week, leading to a slight increase in spot prices after reaching historic lows at the end of April. However, this minor price rise has not alleviated the overall market sentiment, which remains burdened by consistently low demand and high inventory levels at downstream buyers' facilities. These negative factors, combined with broader macroeconomic challenges and specific market pressures, have kept prices under downward pressure for several months, bringing spot values to levels reminiscent of the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020. Nonetheless, some market participants are expressing cautious optimism, suggesting that the market may have reached its lowest point and are hopeful for a gradual recovery in prices as market fundamentals start to stabilize.FAS Houston PVC export prices were assessed at USD 635-645/mt, up USD (+10/mt) from the previous week.Spot feedstock VCM prices on Friday were assessed at the USD 460-470/mt FOB US Gulf levels while FOB US Gulf export EDC prices were assessed at the USD 115-125/mt levels, both dropped by USD (-15/mt) from last week.Spot upstream ethylene prices on Thursday were assessed at 19.50-20.00 cents/lb FOB U.S. Gulf, a week on week increase of (+1 cent/lb).In Asia, PVC prices were assessed at USD 660-700/mt CFR China, unchanged from the previous week.
